2026-04-23T05:16:32.3926944Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T05:16:32.3927384Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T05:16:32.3927937Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T05:16:32.3928250Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T05:16:32.3928482Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-23T05:19:33.3576881Z      - "Key concepts" section only has a brief paragraph about declarative provisioning - doesn't explain what Application Gateway for Containers (AGfC) is, doesn't explain what Traffic Controllers, Frontends, Associations, or SecurityPolicies are
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3578290Z      - "Examples" section only has one example - creating a Traffic Controller with Frontend and Association. Missing example for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3579295Z      - "Next steps" section also has a heading but NO content
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3579589Z   
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3580537Z   2. The snippet in the README (`C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic`) is already defined in `BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs`, but it's not a `[Test]` method or a `Samples/` test file - it's a helper method. The snippet should ideally be in a dedicated Sample file under `tests/Samples/`
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3581550Z   
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3582520Z   3. CHANGELOG mentions 4 resource types: `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, `TrafficControllerAssociation`, and `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. Only the first three are covered in the examples.
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3583473Z   
2026-04-23T05:19:33.3586414Z   Looking at the documentation gaps more broadly, the key issues are the empty "Authenticate the Client" and "Next steps" sections, missing explanations for all four resource types in a "Key concepts" section, no example for `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`, and the sample code living in a test helper instead of a dedicated `tests/Samples/` file. This looks like a new package that needs proper documentation. I should file an issue for these gaps, and I can trace this back to commit `60f88fae603583e41b93d04809c30a100f953fcd`. You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Traffic Controller\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create an Azure Application Gateway for Containers (Traffic Controller).\n\n```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic\nInfrastructure infra = new();\n\nTrafficController controller =\n    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\n    {\n        Tags = { [\"enviro

  "version": "1",
  "description": "Effective Tokens (ET) computation data per the gh-aw Effective Tokens Specification v0.2.0. Token class weights are applied first to normalize across token classes, then the per-model multiplier scales the result relative to the reference model.",
  "reference_model": "claude-sonnet-4.5",
  "token_class_weights": {
    "input": 1.0,
